title: Monday's slow page load and similar rendering issues

posts.author: RaymondChin (Raymond Chin)
posts.cookedHtml:
My team and I are beginning to get really frustrated with monday’s major speed problem.

We’re managing several thousand contacts with roughly 60-70 columns, so our contact board loads very slowly.
Even worse, is when we want to open a contact’s details, the item board takes >25 seconds to fully load. We use this view to see everything going on with the client at once as they may be part of ~7 different types of deals at once, so we’ll need to either update or reference those deals via the “Connected boards” widget in the item board.

A growing frustration is that any updates made will either not take effect at all, take several seconds to reflect in the Connected Boards widget, or just never show up in the widget and we have to refresh to see the changes. Given the slow loading times, this only exacerbates the issue.

It feels even worse when we’re in the middle of a call with a client and have to deal with the slowness. If we were to take call notes outside of Monday and put them in post-call,
that would still eat up our agents time with what should have been possible during-call.

I’ve found Monday to be robust in many areas, and it does provide capabilities that we needed and didn’t have before, but the speed issue is actively hampering our workflow.

Does anybody have any tips on how we can speed up our Monday? If we were to fracture our contact board into several smaller boards, is there a way to quickly maintain all the two-way connections we have between our contact board and all our separate deals boards?

posts.author: silverberrytree (Elise)
posts.cookedHtml: Us too! We’ve just been building out the system to replace our matter management software and boy has it slowed down!

posts.author: Terry.Heard (Terry Heard)
posts.cookedHtml: There have been posts by the Monday team over the last couple of weeks about the Rollout of the new MondayDB. This is meant to be helping with speed issues and accounts are being migrated during the month. I’m sure if you have a little patience things will improve greatly soon.

posts.cookedHtml: We have the same issue. Monday, plz upgrade your servers.

posts.author: OE-Tim (Tim Marsden)
posts.cookedHtml:
We too have speed issues with Monday and have roughly the same size system as Raymond’s. He didn’t mention automations, but we run about 300 automations across all of our boards. I have found that the automations are what is slowing our system down.
We are in the process of upgrading to the Enterprise level as that offers your own server area instead of the common server area for Professional or lower levels. Hopefully this works.

posts.author: DamcoSolutions ( Neelam Bhatt)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @RaymondChin !

Thanks for pinpointing this issue! In fact, one of the effective ways to speed up board creation and simplify contact management is through CRM automation. This can be done in your CRM software by creating multiple workflows that are equipped with AI (Artificial Intelligence). Ultimately, the goal of CRM automation is to improve loading times and make workflows smoother. With CRM automation, you can connect multiple boards, so that data gets updated synchronously. This way, you can quickly maintain all the bi-directional connections.

If you’re looking to get instant results, hire monday.com consultants from a recognized service provider to implement CRM automation. With robust CRM automation in place, your team can remain systematized no matter how fast your business is evolving.

Regards

posts.author: NatashaDrouin (Natasha Drouin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Here in 2024, we are still experiencing this issue. Pages take awhile to load, text is deleted as it is typed because it is not registering the key taps, pages stutter and glitch and columns cannot be edited until a full page reload is performed.

If we are hoping to migrate a mass of our operations into Monday, this will need to be improved upon .

posts.author: RThompson (Reinik Thompson)
posts.cookedHtml: This is still an issue. We have to go out and into boards multiple times to show accurate information. Connected columns don’t always load until we go to the other board to “refresh” it. It just seems to get worse and worse.

posts.author: edchoong (Eddie Choong)
posts.cookedHtml: I have the same problem! It’s just not loading at all some days!

posts.author: kasperhusted (Kasper Husted)
posts.cookedHtml:
I am also noticing that performance is still slow after the implementation of monday db 1.1. - in fact, I find large dashboards to be even slower than they were before.

Widgets take a long time to load. And if I start to change settings/filters around too much, they slow down to a crawl - sometimes even crashing Chrome, requiring a hard reset.

On the old engine, dashboards might have required a refresh to show up-to-date (to the second) data, but at least they loaded fairly quickly.

I have also noticed that if I try to do batch actions on Connect Boards columns on boards, that it can take 10+ seconds to update (sometimes resulting in a crash, again, requiring restarting the browser).

posts.author: cbehmer (Carl Behmer)
posts.cookedHtml: Been trying to deal with this for months, the more we use Monday that slower is gets, we even upgraded to enterprise but no difference has been seen, screens load slow, changes sometimes get lost while things are buffering, this is across different platforms and team members across the US.

posts.author: TFaust (Timo Faust)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have a board with Docs, where I track meeting minutes.

After 20 meetings the page takes half a minute to load, sometimes it does not load correctly.

Also, typing on that page is a chore, as there is a severe lag of up to seconds when typing.

Monday really needs to up their game with the performance issues or optimization for browsers.

posts.author: christopherrobin1024 (Alex)
posts.cookedHtml: From a frontend developer’s perspective, any interface with 70 columns and various data connections will lag. However, the real issue here is the infinite scroll on the contact list—it’s not scalable. We add over 200 records daily, and it becomes a nightmare. Why don’t developers implement pagination, which is much more user-friendly and efficient in terms of resource and memory usage?

posts.author: jure (Jure)
posts.cookedHtml: I am also noticing, that the monday is geting slower and slower. We have quite a complex work, with a lot of items and project, and the speed is starting to bother be. I am just deciding if we go full in in monday, but now I am starting to worry that when we will transfer, it will start to lag too much.

posts.author: maryPPHC (Mary)
posts.cookedHtml: We are new to Monday and have been using it for several months now, similar account size to the OP with <100 automations for now, but that will soon be 250+. The time it takes pages to load is extremely detrimental to our business, and even worse, many times a day Monday.com will freeze if I click to another browser tab and then return to the Monday.com tab, it’s frozen and I have to refresh the entire page, which then takes 30+ seconds to load. Very difficult when you’re on a call with a client. After migrating to Monday.com from Salesforce, we’re very happy overall it offers a lot of great benefits over Salesforce but the speed and freezing issues nearly negate that and almost makes me miss Salesforce…I really hope the Monday team is prioritizing this issue.

posts.author: shayan.moussawi (Shayan Moussawi)
posts.cookedHtml:
Have you tried removing unnecessary columns in the views?

I‘ve had super fast page loads ever since about a week.

However if you have 20+ columns visible it can get difficult

posts.author: jauerbach123 (James Auerbach)
posts.cookedHtml: We have an extensively custom built CRM and it is slow to fully load at times. I am NOT sure if this is due to the number of columns and subitems, or due to the formulas running (or both). Either way Monday needs to find a way to make page loading quicker in general.

posts.author: alexisk (Alexis)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree, it is painfully slow. Each time I reach out to support, they just tell me to clear my cache which never resolves anything. It’s not reliable and incredibly buggy, which is limiting user adoption across our org.

posts.author: LaShaye (LaShaye Floyd)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’ve just submitted an issue to the forum about the system lagging. It happened when I was working in a table column which had about 50 drop down items. I don’t select all 50 items at once because it depends on the campaign. Sometimes 5 items are selected from the drop down list, sometimes it’d 20 items that apply. The system would just freeze and not move. I would have to navigate to another board or even close the browser to refresh the data. Very annoying and time consuming. I didn’t clear cache yet, although that was recommended. Hope I don’t have to clear cache each time. Good luck with your issues - hope you got everything resolved!
LaShaye

posts.author: BruceGosk (Bruce Gosk)
posts.cookedHtml:
Troubleshooting steps for a slow monday account:

  1. do a hard refresh on browser (Ctrl+Shift+R)
  2. click through each board/dashboard/doc and let it load
  3. run a speed test
  4. if you can’t use ethernet make sure you are using the 5GHz channel instead of 2.4GHz, and your wifi adapter is plugged into the USB 3.0 port (if you have one)
  5. clear cache and cookies
  6. use chrome, firefox, safari, or microsoft edge browsers
  7. deactivate any automations that currently aren’t in use
  8. clean up your board data, and delete any redundant columns
  9. upgrade your laptop or PC if its greater than 5 years old, or has very low specs
  10. update your PC/Laptop
  11. update your modem
  12. restart your modem
  13. call your ISP
  14. migrate your account to a closer server
  15. lastly (I know its not possible for everyone) consider upgrading to enterprise as “monday.com provides 99.9% uptime commitment for the Core Service as well as some priority support features to its Enterprise Plan”

Would love to know if anyone else has more tips on this topic!

Bruce Gosk | bruce@mintconsulting.org

posts.author: lou.es (Lou)
posts.cookedHtml: We have the same problem here. EVerything is so slow. We cannot work properly

posts.author: peter15682 (Peter Lemaire)
posts.cookedHtml: One thing that I found is that when you are doing a lot of memory intensive operations, Monday gets slow and stays slow. It gets better when I close the browser and gets even better when I restart my computer, so there might be some kind of high load caching occurring in the background. Monday also seems to do a lot of client-side calculations, so this is also a case where having more/better CPU will also be helpful, rather than it being an issue with server/internet space or responses. At the very least, I have seen great performance differences on PC vs. on a laptop. It might not be the most satisfactory solution, as not everyone can just switch up their hardware, but if you have a critical need for Monday to work as well as possible for a particular instance/presentation/whatever, get yourself on the highest performing machine you can.

posts.author: inspiredearth (Jonathan)
posts.cookedHtml:
Two years and three months later, and my client’s team has a very similar experience. . The Monday.com board and card load times and performance issues are deplorable.

This is long after the DB upgrade Monday deployed, so I hate to think what it was like before that "upgrade.

These are not massive boards. A Contacts board with around ~2000 items. A Jobs (Deals) board with a few hundred items.

Very minor automations (none that are heavy hitters), as we ended up handling any significant automations via a third-party service.

Monday needs to do something about this. I’ve had a performance related ticket open for nearly two months, and all I have received is a lot of updates from a team member saying the dev team are still looking into it.

posts.author: tom.hsu (Tom Hsu)
posts.cookedHtml: And here we are still… Monday.com is consistently being slow. “Monday.com is having the case of the Monday’s”.

posts.author: valentin93 (Valentina Donaghy)
posts.cookedHtml: My board has 70 items and it takes forever to load.

posts.author: valentin93 (Valentina Donaghy)
posts.cookedHtml: I am experiencing this and it wastes so much time in my day-to-day.

posts.author: popjaco (Poppy)
posts.cookedHtml: We’re seeing this too - even worse on WorkDocs. Those things take forever to render

posts.author: nichcraft (Wahed)
posts.cookedHtml: Dealing with this too. The more boards are built, the slower everything gets. You lose too much time just waiting for things to load

posts.author: SwenDekker (Swen Dekker)
posts.cookedHtml:
Been investigating an issue where users have switch to another browser tab (different website) and then switch back after some time to an unresponsive Monday browser tab.
The only solution usually is to close the tab and reopen a new Monday tab.

Tracking the exact cause is not bringing up much results and since I have trouble reproducing it consistently, it’'s been driving me crazy and it has costed me a lot of time already.
Worse thing is, a lot of our users have been experiencing this issue almost every day, causing the overall happiness with Monday as our CRM to dwindle fast.
